agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, with a defined billing cycle and tier."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect subscription fees. Has a status (valid/failed)."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when payment is collected."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automatic attempt to charge the payment method and extend the subscription for another billing cycle."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to collect payment during the renewal window. Up to 3 attempts for standard subscribers."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A time window after failed payment during which the subscription remains active before cancellation. 14 days for Premium tier."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives enhanced cancellation protection via a 14-day grace period."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A time-limited subscription requiring no payment, limited to one active trial per email address."},{"term":"Cancelled Subscription","definition":"A terminal state. The subscription is ended and cannot be reactivated; the customer must create a new one."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription with a known-bad payment method would create a corrupted state — the system would immediately owe service to a customer it cannot bill. This is a data consistency gate, not a policy preference. No VP would approve 'let people activate with broken payment' because it guarantees revenue loss from day one.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer with a payment method marked as failed, WHEN they attempt to activate a subscription, THEN the activation MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy choice — a tunable window designed to allow retries before service interruption. A product or finance team could change this to 5 days or 10 days without corrupting system state. The system would still function correctly with a different window; it is an operational parameter, not a consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a subscription with a billing cycle ending on date D, WHEN the current date reaches D minus 7 days, THEN the system MUST initiate an auto-renewal attempt."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-attempt threshold is a business policy. Management could change this to 2 or 5 attempts based on cost-of-retry vs. churn analysis. The number is a configurable policy lever, not a physical or data-integrity constraint. Note: for Premium tier subscribers, this rule is superseded by BR-5's grace period.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a standard (non-Premium) subscription with 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts, WHEN the third attempt fails, THEN the subscription MUST be automatically transitioned to cancelled status."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. 'Cancelled' is defined as a terminal state. Allowing reactivation would violate the subscription lifecycle model, create ambiguity in billing history (which period does the reactivation cover?), and potentially corrupt audit trails and revenue recognition. The business explicitly chose terminal cancellation to maintain clean subscription lineage. Changing this would require a fundamental redesign of the state machine, not a policy tweak.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a subscription in cancelled status, WHEN any actor attempts to reactivate it, THEN the system MUST reject the reactivation and require creation of a new subscription."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day duration and the Premium-tier-specific treatment are both business policy decisions. The grace period length is a tunable parameter tied to a pricing/retention strategy. A VP could change this to 21 days or extend it to other tiers without breaking system integrity. It is a tier-specific policy benefit, not a data consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal, WHEN 14 days have NOT yet elapsed since the first failure, THEN the subscription MUST remain active. WHEN the 14-day grace period expires without successful payment, THEN the subscription MUST be cancelled."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a uniqueness/data integrity constraint. Allowing multiple simultaneous trials per email would represent a corrupted state — it enables abuse of trial benefits, distorts conversion metrics, and means the system's data model is inconsistent (two records claim to be 'the one active trial' for the same identity). This is analogous to a unique constraint in a database: the system is in an invalid state if violated, regardless of business policy preferences.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an email address that already has an active free trial subscription, WHEN a request is made to create another free trial for the same email, THEN the system MUST reject the request."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer whose only payment method on file has a 'failed' status","when":"The customer attempts to activate a new subscription","then":"The system rejects the activation with a clear error indicating the payment method must be valid"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending on date D","when":"The current date is D minus 7 days","then":"The system initiates an automatic renewal charge attempt against the payment method on file"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard-tier subscription that has accumulated 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts","when":"The third renewal attempt fails","then":"The subscription is automatically transitioned to 'cancelled' status and the customer is notified"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in 'cancelled' status","when":"Any request is made to reactivate or resume that subscription","then":"The system rejects the request and instructs the customer to create a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium-tier subscription with a failed renewal attempt","when":"The first failure occurs and fewer than 14 days have elapsed","then":"The subscription remains active in a 'grace period' state with no service interruption"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A Premium-tier subscription in grace period","when":"14 days elapse without a successful payment","then":"The subscription is automatically cancelled"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A Premium-tier subscription in grace period","when":"A successful payment is collected within the 14-day window","then":"The subscription returns to normal active status and the grace period resets"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An email address with an existing active free trial","when":"A request is made to create a second free trial for the same email","then":"The system rejects the request with a message indicating only one active trial is allowed per email"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An email address whose previous free trial has expired or been cancelled","when":"A request is made to create a new free trial for the same email","then":"The system's behavior is undefined by current spec — see escalation regarding trial re-eligibility"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer updates their payment method to a valid one after activation was rejected","expected_behavior":"The customer should be able to retry activation immediately with the now-valid payment method."},{"scenario":"Renewal window overlaps with a payment method being updated","expected_behavior":"If the payment method becomes valid between retry attempts, the next retry should succeed and the subscription should renew normally."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's grace period coincides with a billing cycle boundary","expected_behavior":"The grace period takes precedence — no new renewal attempt should be initiated while the previous cycle's grace period is still active."},{"scenario":"Customer downgrades from Premium to Standard tier during a grace period","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — does the 14-day grace period persist (grandfathered) or immediately switch to 3-strike logic? Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Free trial user creates a paid subscription then tries to start another trial","expected_behavior":"Should be allowed — the rule constrains active trials, and converting to paid means the trial is no longer active."},{"scenario":"All payment methods are removed (none on file) during an active subscription","expected_behavior":"Renewal attempt should fail, triggering the retry/grace period logic as if the payment method failed."},{"scenario":"Third renewal attempt fails but payment succeeds moments later via webhook","expected_behavior":"Race condition — if cancellation has already been committed, BR-4 means it cannot be reversed. System should handle this with careful ordering or a short processing buffer."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct enforcement of these rules directly protects revenue. BR-1 prevents onboarding customers who cannot pay. BR-2/BR-3 balance retry costs against churn. BR-5's grace period for Premium reduces involuntary churn on the highest-value segment. BR-6 prevents trial abuse that erodes conversion rates.","operational":"The 7-day renewal window and 3-attempt limit define the retry operations cadence. Premium grace periods add a second cancellation pathway that must be tracked separately. Trial uniqueness requires real-time lookups against email indices.","user":"Customers experience predictable billing behavior. Premium subscribers get meaningful protection against accidental payment failures. The terminal cancellation rule (BR-4) may frustrate customers who expect to simply 'turn it back on' — customer support must be prepared.","risk":"The terminal cancellation rule (BR-4) combined with race conditions around the third failed attempt (BR-3) creates a risk of involuntary permanent loss of subscription state. The Premium grace period override of the 3-strike rule must be implemented as a clean branching path, not a bolted-on exception, to avoid logic errors.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard tier)","impact":"Subject to 3-strike cancellation — risk of involuntary churn if payment issues aren't resolved quickly."},{"group":"Customers (Premium tier)","impact":"Protected by 14-day grace period — significantly more time to resolve payment issues before cancellation."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle complaints about terminal cancellation (BR-4) and guide customers through creating new subscriptions."},{"group":"Finance/Revenue Team","impact":"Renewal timing and retry logic directly affect cash flow predictability and involuntary churn metrics."},{"group":"Product/Growth Team","impact":"Trial uniqueness constraint affects acquisition funnel design and re-engagement campaigns."}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The spec is clear on the core rules but leaves several important edge cases unaddressed: trial re-eligibility after expiration, tier downgrade during grace period, and the exact interplay between Premium grace period and the retry mechanism. These gaps prevent full confidence.","high_confidence_areas":["Subscription activation requires valid payment method (BR-1)","Cancelled is a terminal state (BR-4)","One active trial per email uniqueness constraint (BR-6)","7-day pre-renewal window (BR-2)","3-strike cancellation for standard tier (BR-3)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether a customer whose trial expired can ever start another trial (re-eligibility not specified)","How Premium grace period interacts with retry attempts — are retries still attempted during the 14 days, or is it purely time-based?","Tier change during grace period behavior","Whether BR-1 applies only at activation or also at renewal time (renewal with a failed method is handled by retry logic, but the boundary is not explicit)"],"assumptions":["Premium grace period replaces the 3-strike rule entirely rather than supplementing it","The 'failed payment method' check in BR-1 refers to the status at activation time, not a predictive check","'Active trial per email' means the trial subscription is in active status, not merely existing in the system","Trial uniqueness is enforced per email address, not per customer account (if those differ)"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says Premium subscribers get a '14-day grace period instead of the 3-strike rule' but does not clarify whether retry attempts still occur during those 14 days or if it is purely a calendar-based wait. Are there retries during the grace period, and if one succeeds, does the grace period end?","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering","Premium customers","Finance"],"options":["A) Grace period is purely time-based — no retries during the 14 days, single check at expiry","B) Retries still occur during the 14-day window (e.g., daily), and any success ends the grace period","C) A hybrid: retries occur, but cancellation is only triggered by time expiry, not attempt count"],"recommendation":"Option B — retry during grace period with early success exit. This maximizes payment recovery while preserving the premium benefit of extended time."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Free trial rule states 'cannot have more than one active trial per email at any time' but does not specify whether a customer whose trial has ended (expired or cancelled) is eligible for another trial in the future.","affected_stakeholders":["Product/Growth","Customers","Engineering"],"options":["A) One trial per email, ever — lifetime uniqueness","B) One active trial at a time — re-eligibility after the previous trial ends","C) Configurable cooldown period between trials"],"recommendation":"Option A — one trial per email ever. This is industry standard to prevent trial abuse and simplifies implementation. If re-engagement is needed, use promotional offers instead."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No specification for what happens if a Premium subscriber downgrades to Standard tier during an active grace period. Does the 14-day grace period continue, or does the account immediately switch to the 3-strike rule?","affected_stakeholders":["Premium customers","Customer Support","Engineering"],"options":["A) Grace period is grandfathered — the 14-day window continues since it was earned under Premium","B) Immediate switch to 3-strike logic, counting from zero","C) Immediate switch to 3-strike logic, counting existing failures"],"recommendation":"Option A — honor the grace period that was active when the failure occurred. Changing rules mid-stream would feel punitive and generate support tickets."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

This is analogous to a unique constraint in a database: the system is in an invalid state if violated, regardless of business policy preferences.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an email address that already has an active free trial subscription, WHEN a request is made to create another free trial for the same email, THEN the system MUST reject the
